text:
HEMATOPATHOLOGY / BONE MARROW BIOPSY REPORT

DEPARTMENT OF PATHOLOGY AND LABORATORY MEDICINE
HEMATOPATHOLOGY DIVISION

---

SPECIMEN INFORMATION

Patient: Renata Vasquez-Okonkwo
Date of Birth: [on file]
MRN: [on file]
Accession Number: HP-[REDACTED]
Ordering Physician: Hematology-Oncology / Bone Marrow Transplant Service
Specimen Received: [date not recorded in this report]
Report Date: Day +30 post-allogeneic HSCT

CLINICAL HISTORY / INDICATION

48-year-old female with ELN 2022 Adverse-risk acute myeloid leukemia (AML), FAB M1 morphology, with FLT3-ITD high allelic ratio (0.91), trisomy 8, and complex co-mutation profile (DNMT3A R882H, TET2 Q1299*, WT1 R462W, NRAS Q61H, KDM6A, STAG2, BCOR), NPM1 wild-type. Presented initially with WBC 87,400/uL, 74% marrow blasts, and active gingival bleeding. Achieved MRD-negative complete remission (CR) after 7+3 induction chemotherapy plus midostaurin. Proceeded to myeloablative conditioning (fludarabine/busulfan FluBu4) followed by matched unrelated donor (MUD, 10/10 HLA allele-matched, 34-year-old male) allogeneic peripheral blood stem cell transplantation (allo-PBSCT), CD34+ cell dose 6.2 x 10^6 cells/kg. Day +21 peripheral blood chimerism demonstrated 99% overall donor engraftment, 100% myeloid chimerism. Bone marrow biopsy now submitted for day +30 engraftment and disease reassessment.

---

SPECIMEN(S) SUBMITTED

  A.  Bone marrow core biopsy (right posterior superior iliac crest; 1.6 cm core received)
  B.  Bone marrow aspirate (right posterior superior iliac crest; smears and particle clot sections submitted)
  C.  Peripheral blood sample for STR chimerism analysis (submitted concurrently; results incorporated herein)

---

GROSS DESCRIPTION

Specimen A: Received in formalin, a cylindrical core biopsy measuring 1.6 cm in length x 0.2 cm in diameter. The specimen is sectioned and entirely submitted for decalcification and paraffin embedding. Representative sections at three levels are submitted for hematoxylin and eosin staining and immunohistochemistry as noted below.

Specimen B: Aspirate smears (8 slides prepared at bedside, 2 air-dried for Wright-Giemsa staining, 2 fixed in methanol for Prussian blue staining) and a particle clot section (formalin-fixed, paraffin-embedded). The aspirate is adequate for evaluation; spicules are present. Differential count performed on Wright-Giemsa-stained smears, 500 nucleated cells counted.

Specimen C: Peripheral blood (4 mL EDTA) submitted for short tandem repeat (STR) chimerism analysis. Whole blood and CD3+ (T-cell) and CD33+ (myeloid) lineage-separated fractions analyzed.

---

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION

Core Biopsy (Specimen A):

Sections of the core biopsy demonstrate a moderately cellular marrow with estimated overall cellularity of 40-50% (appropriate for age and post-transplant day). There is active trilineage hematopoiesis with representation of erythroid, granulocytic, and megakaryocytic lineages. The erythroid precursors show normal maturation without dyserythropoietic features; normoblastic erythropoiesis is present. Granulopoiesis shows orderly left-shifted maturation with a spectrum of myeloblasts, promyelocytes, myelocytes, metamyelocytes, and mature forms. The proportion of myeloblasts is not increased by morphologic assessment (see aspirate differential below). Megakaryocytes are present in adequate numbers (approximately 4-6 per 10x field on average), with predominantly normal morphology; rare forms show hypolobated nuclei, possibly reflecting the early post-engraftment period. No clusters of abnormal blasts, no sheets of immature cells, and no evidence of infiltration by an abnormal non-hematopoietic population are identified. The intertrabecular background shows residual edema and scattered mast cells, consistent with the early post-transplant healing marrow microenvironment. Bony trabeculae are of normal thickness and architecture. No necrosis. No granulomas.

Ironically, compared to the presenting marrow (estimated cellularity 95% with 74% blasts), the current biopsy reflects the expected post-engraftment reconstitution pattern.

Immunohistochemistry:
  - CD34: Scattered CD34+ cells identified, comprising less than 3% of marrow cellularity; distribution is interstitial/sinusoidal, consistent with normal endothelial staining and rare progenitors. No clusters of CD34+ blasts.
  - CD117 (c-Kit): Scattered mast cells positive; no abnormal c-Kit-positive blast clusters.
  - MPO: Stains granulocytic precursors appropriately along full maturation spectrum; no abnormal MPO-positive immature population.
  - CD20 / CD3: T-lymphocytes and B-lymphocytes are present in normal proportions; no lymphoid aggregates or lymphoproliferative infiltrate.
  - CD61: Megakaryocytes highlighted appropriately; number and distribution normal for day +30 post-transplant.
  - Ki-67 (MIB-1): Proliferative index in hematopoietic elements is moderately elevated (approximately 20-25%), consistent with active post-engraftment regeneration.

Aspiration Smears and Particle Clot (Specimen B):

The aspirate is adequate and well-spread. Particle clot sections show hypercellular particles with active hematopoiesis, concordant with the biopsy.

Aspiration differential count (500 nucleated cells):
  Myeloblasts:                  2.4%
  Promyelocytes:                4.8%
  Myelocytes:                   9.6%
  Metamyelocytes:               8.2%
  Bands/neutrophils:           22.4%
  Eosinophils:                  2.0%
  Basophils:                    0.4%
  Lymphocytes:                 11.2%
  Monocytes:                    4.6%
  Erythroid precursors (all):  34.2%
    (Pronormoblasts 0.8%, basophilic normoblasts 3.4%, polychromatophilic normoblasts 18.6%, orthochromatic normoblasts 11.4%)
  Plasma cells:                 0.2%
  Unclassifiable:               0.0%
  M:E ratio: approximately 1.9:1 (within normal limits)

Blast morphology: The 2.4% myeloblasts identified are small to medium-sized with round to slightly irregular nuclei, fine chromatin, 1-2 small nucleoli, and scant to moderate agranular cytoplasm. Auer rods are NOT identified in the current specimen. Morphology is consistent with normal regenerating myeloblasts; no cytologic features of dysplasia, abnormal granulation, or nuclear hypersegmentation to suggest MDS-type changes. No binucleate or multinucleate erythroid forms.

Prussian Blue iron stain: Adequate iron stores present. Ring sideroblasts: absent (0 of 100 erythroid precursors examined).

---

ANCILLARY STUDIES

1. FLOW CYTOMETRY MINIMAL RESIDUAL DISEASE (MRD) ASSESSMENT
   Laboratory: [Institutional Flow Cytometry Laboratory]
   Platform: 10-color multiparameter flow cytometry
   Assay sensitivity: 0.01% (1 in 10,000 events)
   Total events acquired: 200,000 (bone marrow aspirate)

   The specimen was interrogated using a panel optimized for AML MRD detection, incorporating the leukemia-associated immunophenotype (LAIP) defined at diagnosis: CD34+/CD117+/CD13+/CD33+/CD123+/HLA-DR+, with dim-to-absent CD38 and aberrant CD123 co-expression.

   RESULT: MRD NEGATIVE

   No population with the previously defined LAIP was detected above the assay threshold. Normal hematopoietic progenitors are present with appropriate antigenic expression and maturation patterns. No aberrant antigen co-expression or asynchronous maturation consistent with residual AML blasts is identified at the level of sensitivity of this assay (0.01%).

2. MOLECULAR MRD -- FLT3-ITD PCR
   Laboratory: [Molecular Diagnostics Laboratory]
   Method: Quantitative PCR / fragment analysis for FLT3-ITD (33-bp insertion, exon 14, as characterized at diagnosis)
   Specimen: Bone marrow aspirate (mononuclear cell fraction)
   Assay sensitivity: 1 in 10,000 (10^-4)

   RESULT: FLT3-ITD NOT DETECTED
   FLT3-ITD allelic ratio: < limit of detection
   The specific 33-bp ITD insertion documented at diagnosis is undetectable in this specimen at the reported assay sensitivity.

3. WT1 QUANTITATIVE PCR
   Method: Quantitative RT-PCR for WT1 transcript (ELN-standardized assay)
   Specimen: Bone marrow aspirate
   Reference: Normalized to ABL1 housekeeping gene

   RESULT: WT1 = 18 copies / 10^4 ABL1 copies
   Reference range (normal marrow / post-transplant): < 50 copies / 10^4 ABL1 copies
   Interpretation: Within normal limits. Not elevated. Consistent with normal regenerating marrow without AML-related WT1 overexpression.

4. STR CHIMERISM ANALYSIS (SHORT TANDEM REPEAT)
   Laboratory: [Molecular HLA / Chimerism Laboratory]
   Method: PCR-based STR amplification with capillary electrophoresis
   Specimen: Peripheral blood (Specimen C; concurrent with marrow sampling, day +30)
   Baseline samples: Pre-transplant recipient sample and donor sample on file.

   RESULTS:

   Whole blood (unsorted):
     % Donor:     99.4%
     % Recipient: 0.6%

   CD3+ T-cell fraction:
     % Donor:     95.2%
     % Recipient: 4.8%

   CD33+ Myeloid fraction:
     % Donor:     100.0%
     % Recipient:  0.0% (below assay detection threshold of 1%)

   Interpretation: Near-complete to complete donor engraftment. Complete myeloid chimerism (100% donor in CD33+ fraction). Predominantly donor T-cell chimerism with small residual recipient T-cell fraction (4.8%), which is not uncommon at day +30 following myeloablative conditioning in the context of short-course Trexall GVHD prophylaxis and is not in itself indicative of graft failure or impending relapse. No evidence of mixed chimerism in the myeloid compartment suggestive of recipient-origin hematopoietic recovery or residual/relapsing leukemia.

---

DIAGNOSTIC SUMMARY

1. BONE MARROW CORE BIOPSY AND ASPIRATE (day +30 post-myeloablative allo-PBSCT, matched unrelated donor):
   - Moderately cellular marrow (40-50% cellularity) with complete, morphologically normal trilineage hematopoiesis.
   - Myeloblasts 2.4% by 500-cell aspirate differential; no morphologic evidence of residual or recurrent acute myeloid leukemia.
   - Auer rods absent.
   - No dysplastic features.
   - Iron stores adequate; no ring sideroblasts.

2. FLOW CYTOMETRY MRD: NEGATIVE (sensitivity 0.01%).
   No aberrant immunophenotypic blast population detected.

3. MOLECULAR MRD (FLT3-ITD PCR): NOT DETECTED (sensitivity 10^-4).

4. WT1 QUANTITATIVE PCR: 18 copies/10^4 ABL1 (within normal limits).

5. STR CHIMERISM (peripheral blood, day +30):
   - Overall: 99.4% donor
   - Myeloid (CD33+): 100% donor
   - T-cell (CD3+): 95.2% donor
   Consistent with robust engraftment and full myeloid donor chimerism.

OVERALL CONCLUSION: Complete remission with MRD negativity by multiparameter flow cytometry and FLT3-ITD molecular PCR assay. Full myeloid donor chimerism. No morphologic, immunophenotypic, or molecular evidence of residual acute myeloid leukemia at day +30 post-transplant.

---

DIAGNOSING PATHOLOGIST: [Attending Hematopathologist, MD, PhD]
ELECTRONIC SIGNATURE: Verified and released

---

NOTE: This report encompasses results from bone marrow morphology, immunohistochemistry, flow cytometry MRD, FLT3-ITD molecular PCR, WT1 quantitative PCR, and peripheral blood STR chimerism. Clinical correlation is recommended. Results communicated to the bone marrow transplant service at time of preliminary review.
